mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a healthy labor market, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative on average during periods when UNRATE is below 5.5% and stable (std dev < 0.1 over 3 months).

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above a certain threshold signals aggressive monetary tightening, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the FEDFUNDS rate exceeding the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust industrial production (INDPRO) indicates broad-based economic expansion, suggesting healthy corporate revenues and profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO show strong growth as per the trigger, SPX 20-day returns are flat or negative on average over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) during an economic downturn signals deepening recessionary pressures, as housing is a key cyclical sector, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: During identified recessionary periods, a decline in HOUST does not correlate with negative SPX returns, or SPX returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) provides a favorable environment for corporate planning and consumer purchasing power, reducing uncertainty and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no significant positive correlation with stable, low CPIAUCSL readings (annualized change < 2.5% and 3-month std dev < 0.1) over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration or contraction in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a weakening labor market, signaling reduced consumer demand and potential recession, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Periods of negative PAYEMS growth are followed by positive or flat 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.0 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a robust economy, which is highly favorable for corporate profits and S&P 500 performance over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy in growth environment
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is low and GDPC1 growth is strong, S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently negative or flat, showing no statistically significant positive average return.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) following a period of contraction signals a recovery in the manufacturing sector and broader economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon, especially during post-recession recoveries.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth turns positive after 3 consecutive months of negative growth
failure_condition: Following a confirmed rebound in INDPRO after a contraction, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not statistically significant.
